title: eupatorium gracile var. typicum
intro: Eupatorium gracile var. typicum, also known as the slender joe-pye weed, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for colds, coughs, and sore throats. In herbal preparations, Eupatorium gracile var. typicum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
